name: Astronormanical Atlas (Deck Guide)
description_md: # ** AstroNormanical Atlas ** *Illus. Jesse Mead* **Clue: 9 | Enemy Mgmt: 5 | Treachery: 7.5 | Tempo: 10 | Consistency: 10** It's a running joke that Norman is a professor at the Miskatonic University without access to [Higher Education](/card/02187) or [Miskatonic Archaeology Funding](/card/60232). Up until this point, you had to roleplay being a pariah with little *academic* support, by playing with little to no *card* support. Not anymore. It's our turn to shine, and we dazzle like a star in the night sky. Our destiny as one of the most powerful investigators in the game has finally revealed itself. Here's why: - **Star Searcher**: Most investigators have 1 copy of their Signature card. Norman has effectively 3 copies. [Livre d'Eibon](/card/08005) is searchable via [Research Librarian](/card/01032) meaning you a 72% chance of seeing in your opening hand (assuming you take a 5 card mulligan.) If we factor in the other card that's effectively Norman's signature, [Astronomical Atlas](/card/08067), you have a **93%** chance of seeing at least 1 of the aforementioned cards in your opening hand. - **Early Powerspike & High Tempo**: Norman needs 3 XP to get rolling. That's IT. Once you get your first copy of Astronomical Atlas, you are already a powerhouse. Also, you play very few assets and as a result, you require very little setup. In fact with the new neutral permanent, In the Thick of It, you can start out with 1 Atlas in your deck. - **Hyper Cycle**: Thankfully, you still have your Seeker roots. You will cycle through your deck extremely quickly, letting you troubleshoot the game by effectively playing the game with your whole deck at your fingertips. - **Self-Reliant Cluever**: As a primary cluever for 4 players, you are still self-sufficient when it comes to dealing with sporadic enemies. Mind of Matter and Promise of Power help you pass critical scenario-based tests in all 4 stats. ----------- ### **[Click Here for 0 EXP Deck (Option 1)](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/1649590)** ### **[Click Here for 3 EXP Deck (Option 2)](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/1843998)** ----------- ### ** Stars & Skills ** Norman's whole strategy plays around the top card of his deck. If the top card of his deck is an asset or an event, he can play it a resource discount once per turn. You can effectively think of him having a free an additional card in his hand as a result of this. Lets discuss a few cards that are relevant for manipulating that top card and pushing its value beyond just 1 additional card. - [Livre d'Eibon](/card/08005): Before this card, Norman was disincentivized from including too many skill cards because they would prevent you from using your ability when they are at the top of your deck. Livre let's you unbrick skill cards and commit them to skill tests. Better yet, you can swap the top card of your deck with a card already in your hand that you want to play at a discount. - [Astronomical Atlas](/card/08067): In addition to Livre helping you unbrick skill cards, Astronomical Atlas lets you skill cards again. When you see a Perception, or Deduction at the top of your deck, use Astro to attach that skill card. Then, when you succeed a skill test in which you commit that skill card, you get to add that skill card to your hand and do it all over again. - [Written in the Stars](/card/08034): Since you can see the top card of your deck, you know exactly what skill card you can discard. This card effectively turns you into [Amanda Sharpe](/card/07002) for the turn. Discard a Deduction from the top of your deck to grab 2 clues per investigation. You can also add 3 charges to powerful spells like [Shrivelling](/card/02306), [Divination](/card/08101), [Rite of Seeking](/card/02233), [Shards of the Void](/card/04310), and [Blur](/card/08109) each time you successfully investigate. Tech cards like [Shortcut](/card/02022) and [Cryptographic Cipher](/card/07021) to maximize the number of actions you take committing the skill card Written discarded. ***Tip!*** - *Normanly when committing to another player's skill tests, you are permitted 1 card. However, because the effects of [Practice Makes Perfect](/card/06197), [Livre d'Eibon](/card/08005), and [Astronomical Atlas](/card/08067) are triggered abilities that let you commit cards, you can use these on top of your 1 card to commit up to 5 cards to another player's skill test.* ----------- ### ** Deck Combos ** ** Setup ** - Play [Research Librarian](/card/01032): Search Livre or Astro Atlas. Trigger Astounding Revelation to refund the cost of playing Research Librarian. This lets you play both tomes in your opening turn total exactly 5 resources. - Prioritize Astro > Livre. Astro duplicates your card draw skill cards and unbricks them just by itself. Livre does not let you use skill cards again, by itself. ** Deduction Loop ** - Astro Atlas: Attach [Deduction](/card/01039) to Astro Atlas. - : **Investigate.** Commit Deduction via the ability on Astro Atlas. After you succeed, add Deduction to your hand. - Livre: Swap the top card of your deck with Deduction so that Deduction is back on top of your deck. - Repeat the loop by attaching the Deduction onto Astro again. Note, this combo takes 2 turns to set up, since Astro needs to be ready again to put Deduction back. Afterwards, the loop will refresh every round. - You can extend this loop with 2 copies of Atlas to bounce the copy of Deduction, netting you 2 uses on a **recurring** Deduction every other round. ( Astro 1 commit Deduction Livre place Deduction on top of deck Attach to Astro 2 Commit Deduction from Astro 2) - Other great cards to consider are [Eureka!](/card/03231) for filtered search, [Perception](/card/01090) for draw, [Fearless](/card/02268) to heal 2 horror every round, [Defiance](/card/04198) to block really bad token effects, [Enraptured](/card/04157) to recharge your ***Spells***. ** Astounding Practice ** - Because you can see the top card, you can use [Practice Makes Perfect](/card/06197) when you see a ***Practiced*** trait skill card to guarantee its effect. - Livre: Swap an [Astounding Revelation](/card/06023) in your hand onto the top of your deck when you have access to [Eureka!](/card/03231) or want to use Practice. Now, Astounding Revelation will never be a dead draw! ** Written in the Stars ** - Livre: Swap a desired skill card from your hand to the top of the deck. - Play [Written in the Stars](/card/08034) to discard that card from the top of your deck. - Play [Shortcut](/card/02022) for moving you to a new location with more clues during this round. - Play [Mind over Matter](/card/01036) to convert all non- tests you take this turn to match the skill card you discarded with Written. - Best targets are [Deduction](/card/01039) for extra clues, [Enraptured](/card/04157) can be considered pseudo-clues as they replenish your Divination, and can also be used to recharge your Blur. ** Willpower Icons ** - We run [Divination](/card/08101) largely for clue compression and giving you the freedom of choosing investigate actions to be either based on or . This helps you find tests to commit [Guts](/card/60424) and [Fearless](/card/02268) to. You can use Written + Enraptured to recharge your Divination back up. - If the charge strategy is too much for you, just play a lvl 4 [Sixth Sense](/card/05322) in this card's place to give you tests to commit to. This also frees up lvl 0 deck space taken up by Enraptured. ***Tip!*** - *This build is playing [St. Hubert's Key](/card/03269) putting Norman's stats at 6 health / 6 sanity. If you end up taking In the Thick of It, 6 health / 4 sanity. Because of how fragile he will be, you absolutely will want to keep your [Deny Existence](/card/05032) for a critical burst of damage or horror that would wipe you. Aggressively seek out the use of the "Deduction" loop with Fearless and bank the card back on an Atlas with Livre as often as you can. [Deny Existence](/card/05280) Lvl 5 upgrade is a really good upgrade to consider, and getting 2 of those would help you with how reckless this deck can be.* ** Foresight ** - This will most likely be subject to change as we don't have rulings on this card. However, it is generally accepted/understood that should you draw a weakness from the top of your deck via your **Forced** ability, you can use [Foresight](/card/08064) to cancel it. Because of this, always save 1 copy of Foresight for your basic weakness. There are 2 copies, the other one can be used to play assets and/or when you start accruing more than just 1 basic weakness in your deck. - Livre: Put an asset or event you want to play on top of your deck from your hand. - : Perform a skill test that you can commit a cantrip like [Perception](/card/01090), [Guts](/card/01089), or [Eureka!](/card/03231) to, in order to draw the top card of your deck. - Play [Foresight](/card/08064) to name that card and play it fast. - At the moment, we do not know what the interaction with [The Harbinger](/card/08006) will be like. You definitely cannot draw Harbinger from the top of your deck because it prevents you. Where it gets tricky is what if you searched Harbinger from your deck via something like [Eureka!](/card/03231), [Mr. "Rook"](/card/05153), or [No Stone Unturned](/card/03026). Many have seen the "search and draw" as a singular effect and can't be interrupted by Foresight choosing to have resolved half of the effect before the other half of the effect. So the least charitable interpretation of Foresight suggests that, calling Harbinger prior to a "search and draw" effect allows you to cancel Harbinger. We don't actually know. So depending on future information, Foresight could become better or worse when it comes to dealing with Harbinger. ----------- ### ** Enemy Management ** * [Occult Lexicon](/card/05316): At lvl 0, you can play this card to help you deal with enemies since you might not have the deck space for fight ***Spells***. You will always have an excess of cards and resources to pay for this effect. * [Shrivelling](/card/01060): If you can manage to squeeze this card into your deck, great. You can recharge this with Enraptured. * [Mind over Matter](/card/01036): Lets you fight and evade using your highest stat. * ["I've got a plan!"](/card/02107) + [Occult Invocation](/card/60217): Options for doing burst damage. Refer to Occult Lexicon for Invocation's discard cost. Play whichever one you prefer, I prefer I've got a plan, so that's why its in this deck. * [Blur](/card/08111): Gives you 1-2 actions back depending on which level you have. [Mists of R'lyeh](/card/53007) works as well, but I don't love the possibility of discarding a card since you often bank your cards in your hand for certain combos. Blur works well with Written discarding Eureka since you can transition from using to evade to investigate with . It also gives you extra actions to deal with Harbinger. ----------- ### ** Upgrade Path ** The more icons, the earlier you need it in a campaign. When upgrading, make sure you have cleared a card with more icons before going to a lower tier if possible. * [Astronomical Atlas](/card/08067): This is a skill-based deck. Astro increases the speed you access cards by changing the top card of your deck, and enables loops, and repeated use of skills. A must first buy. * [Blur](/card/08109) + [Divination](/card/08101): We run a lot of based skills. Fearless is really vital to staying healthy with this build. This gives you proactive ways to take tests. Blur's extra action(s) is so vital for extending [Written in the Stars](/card/08034) and finding actions to use on Harbinger. * [Fearless](/card/02268), [Deny Existence](/card/05280), and [Ward of Protection](/card/03270): This part is all about making you a more robust investigator, so that you don't die prematurely. * [Foresight](/card/08064): Saves you the action of playing over Blur and Divination when you play over the other card as a way to refresh charges and recur it when your deck loops. Also, saves you the trouble of dealing with your basic weakness. * [Defiance](/card/04198), [Guts](/card/60424): Now you can get the better skills. [Seal of the Elder Sign](/card/03312) is on the table for your teammates since your elder effect isn't all that for a skill-based deck. ----------- ### ** Alt Card & Tech Choices ** * [Versatile](/card/06167): A conversation about Versatile. You can offset the bloating of the deck. It also let's you put back in lvl 0 cards after you've already upgraded out of your initial ones. This is a very important usage of Versatile for the Edge of the Earth class-evolving investigators. Also, you can find really useful skills to combo with your engines like [Inspiring Presence](/card/03228), ["Watch this!"](/card/03233), [Quick Thinking](/card/02229), [Leadership](/card/02260). * [Inspiring Presence](/card/03228): Inspiring Presence + Written in the Stars is like at 4 fast hits of [Beat Cop](/card/01028), 4 fast searches of [Arcane Initiate](/card/01063) a round, 4 fast [Mr. "Rook"](/card/05153) searches, 4 [Dr. Milan Christopher](/card/01033) resources generated, 10 [David Renfield](/card/03112) resource generated. Now if you use Blur as your first action... that's up to 5 times you can ready an exhausted _**Ally**_ that round. * [Winging It](/card/04272): This is sort of a meme but you can use Eureka + Written to aggressively dig for this card. Then, when you have Harbinger locking you from manipulating your deck, you can keep on playing Winging It for 2 clues an action until you run out of resources/economy and want to get rid of your Harbinger. * [Pocket Telescope](/card/08097): So, this deck plays Shortcut to minimize the need for you to perform move actions when you have a big Written in the Stars turn. This does a similar thing where you don't have to perform move actions to investigate locations that are connecting and already revealed. This however, works better if you have investigators opening up locations, or if you set it up by walking past a location and gathering clues behind you. * [Shrivelling](/card/01060), [Ward of Protection](/card/01065), and [Inquiring Mind](/card/02227): It is okay to drop St. Hubert Key and Promise of Power for these Mystic staples. If you are not playing Practice Makes Perfect, Promise of Power isn't absolutely necessary. You can play Inquiring Mind as a healthy alternative in order to free up your limited Mystic lvl 0 slots. Shrivelling works particularly well with Enraptured loops when you are asked to fight a little bit.
